grammar.usage: Used to compare two things (comparative) or to compare one thing in a group with all others (superlative).
grammar.example: My new job is more enjoyable than my old one. / Lan is the tallest in our class.

bullets: We use comparatives to compare two different things, especially in sentences with the word **than**.
EX: *My new job is more enjoyable than my old one.*

bullets: Always use the correct comparative form instead of the regular adverb in comparisons.
EX: *We lost the match because we played worse than the other team did.* (✓)
EX: *We lost the match because we played badly than the other team did.* (✗)
We use superlatives to compare one thing in a group with all other things in that group.
The word **the** usually comes before the superlative form.
EX: *Lan is the tallest in our class.*

bullets: Ensure the superlative form of the adjective is used instead of the base or comparative form when modifying the noun.
EX: *Listen! I promise you this is the (funny) joke ever!*
--> *Listen! I promise you this is the funniest joke ever!*

vocabulary.meaning: able to accept or tolerate delays, problems, or suffering without becoming annoyed or anxious
vocabulary.example: You need to be patient when learning a new language.

vocabulary.meaning: sleeping or in a state of sleep
vocabulary.example: He was so tired that he fell asleep during the movie.

vocabulary.meaning: an exhibition, typically with amusement rides and stalls, or a gathering for buying and selling
vocabulary.example: The children had a great time playing games at the summer fair.

vocabulary.meaning: containing strong spices or having a hot flavor
vocabulary.example: This curry is very spicy, so make sure to have some water nearby.

vocabulary.meaning: having little thickness or small distance between opposite sides; or slim/lean
vocabulary.example: He wore a thin jacket because the weather was relatively warm.

vocabulary.meaning: the spirit of a dead person, represented as a pale, transparent image
vocabulary.example: Some people believe that this old castle is haunted by a ghost.
She got a job as a junior accountant at a local law firm.

vocabulary.meaning: covered or marked with an unclean substance; not clean
vocabulary.example: Please put your dirty clothes in the laundry basket.

vocabulary.meaning: a stout-bodied bird with a small head and short legs, common in cities
vocabulary.example: A flock of pigeons gathered in the square to feed on bread crumbs.

vocabulary.meaning: a regular payment made during a person's retirement from an investment fund
vocabulary.example: He retired at the age of sixty-five and is now living on his pension.

vocabulary.meaning: to be worthy of something or have a right to it because of one's actions
vocabulary.example: She worked extremely hard and fully deserves to win the award.

vocabulary.meaning: to make someone feel admiration or respect
vocabulary.example: His excellent command of English impressed the interview panel.
